The origins: who was Saint Reparata? Before the Florentines began the construction of their gigantic duomo in 1296, the smaller church of Santa Reparata served as the city's cathedral. title: Santa Reparata | Opera Duomo, Florence
highlights: stairway**descends to the site of the ancient basilica of Santa Reparata, brought to light during an archaeological campaign lasting from 1965 to 1973**. Here, the visitor experiences a journey back in time, through the construction phases of one of the first Christian temples in the history of Florence. The ancient basilica remained standing and was officiated for more than eight centuries, from its founding early in the fifth century to its demolition in 1379, year of completion of the basilica  ...    early-medieval churches of the Tuscia region: 50 metres (164 feet) in length and 25 metres (82 feet) wide, in triple-nave basilica plan, adorned with an extraordinary mosaic floor in geometric motifs, with Solomonic knots, vases  ...    The site also includes remains from the still earlier Florence of Roman times, and traces of Carolingian renovations to  ...
